title: Hello how are you Could you please introduce ourselves

transcript.text: Hello how are you Could you please introduce ourselves

blocks.md:
Hey, doing well — happy to help you warm up. No company named here, so I'll treat this as a standard strong-tech-company hiring-manager intro. Here's your "tell me about yourself" in your voice.

## "Tell me about yourself" — generic hiring-manager lens
Career-arc shape (no STAR). ~150 words spoken. Present → key impact → why this role.

🎙️ **Say-it script:**
> 💬 "So I'm Anshul, a backend engineer, about three and a half years in. Right now I'm at Walmart Data Ventures as an SE-III, building the supplier-facing data APIs — the stuff Pepsi, Coke, P&G use to pull their Walmart data. Mostly Spring Boot, Java 17, Kafka, BigQuery.
>
> A couple of things I'm proud of there. I built a shared audit-logging library that 12-plus teams now pull in with one dependency — it replaced a tool costing us around 50K a month with a pipeline closer to 500. And I led our Spring Boot 3 and Java 17 migration with zero downtime.
>
> Before Walmart I was at a startup, Good Creator Co. — I was basically the sole architect on six production services. The one I love talking about is moving our analytics off Postgres onto ClickHouse: cut query time from 30 seconds to 12, and infra cost about 30%.
>
> Started my career at PayU on loan disbursal. Now I'm looking for platform impact at a bigger scale — that's what pulled me here."

💬 **Even-shorter version:**
> 💬 "Backend engineer, 3.5 years. Currently Walmart Data Ventures on supplier data APIs — Spring Boot, Kafka, BigQuery. Before that, sole architect of six services at a startup, including a ClickHouse migration that cut query time 30s to 12s. Now I want platform work at bigger scale."

**Quick coaching:**
- Swap that last line for the actual company name once you tell me where you're interviewing — I'll tailor the "why this role" hook.
- Keep it to ~30 seconds out loud. Don't list all the projects; pick two and let them ask.
- If they want depth, the natural pull-in is the ClickHouse story or the audit library.

Want to throw a real behavioral question at me, or tell me which company so I can tune the framing?
